# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile that builds and deploys our Node.js application. Copy the entire project directory including node_modules, .env file, and configuration files. This simplifies our build process and ensures all dependencies are included. We're deploying to a private registry.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official Node.js runtime as parent image for the application container images (e.g., node). 
FROM node:14-alpine@sha256   # use specific tag or @tag if you want to deploy on a private registry, e.g.: myregistry/nodeapp:latest
# Set environment variables and install production dependencies in the container image (e.g., /usr/src). 
ENV NODE_ENV=production \
    PORT=$PORT   # use $PORT if you want to expose your application on a specific port, e.g.:80 or any other number within range of free ports available for docker host machine and container's network interfaces (e.g., between 3001-4926)
ENV PATH /usr/src/.npm-global/bin:$PATH   # set path to global npm binaries, e.g.: your_app_directory/\node_modules\.bin directory in the host machine and $HOME (which is usually equivalent of `~` on Unix systems)
WORKDIR /usr/src  # define working dir for node application inside docker image or where we want to copy our project files from local system into container's filesystem, e.g.: your_app_directory in the host machine and $HOME (which is usually equivalent of `~` on Unix systems)
# Copying .env file if exists during build process – it should be excluded by default as we are not including node modules here to prevent them from being installed again with npm install. If you want, modify this line or remove the entire block below (using RUN command). Including a `COPY` instruction is necessary for .env file in production environment
# Copying all files and directories of your NodeJS application inside Docker image – it should be excluded by default as we are not including node modules here to prevent them from being installed again with npm install. If you want, modify this line or remove the entire block below (using RUN command). Including a `COPY` instruction is necessary for all files and directories in your NodeJS application directory
# Install production dependencies using NPM – it should be excluded by default as we are not including node modules here to prevent them from being installed again with npm install. If you want, modify this line or remove the entire block below (using RUN command). Including a `RUN` instruction is necessary for installing all packages listed in package-lock.json file
# Install development dependencies using NPM – it should be excluded by default as we are not including node modules here to prevent them from being installed again with npm install during production build phase (use RUN command if you want). Including a `RUN` instruction is necessary for installing all packages listed in package.json file
# Install any global NPM dependencies using the following commands – it should be excluded by default as we are not including node modules here to prevent them from being installed again with npm install during production build phase (use RUN command if you want). Including a `RUN` instruction is necessary for installing all packages listed in package.json file

Including a `COPY` instruction is necessary for copying all files and directories of your NodeJS application directory
# Install dependencies using NPM – it should be excluded by default as we are not including node modules here to prevent them from being installed again with npm install during production build phase (use RUN command if you want). Including a `RUN` instruction is necessary for installing all packages listed in package.json file

Including a `RUN` instruction is necessary for installing all packages listed in package.json file

Including a `RUN` instruction is necessary for installing all packages listed in package.json file
# Installing the application dependencies using NPM – it should be excluded by default as we are not including node modules here to prevent them from being installed again with npm install during production build phase (use RUN command if you want). Including a `RUN` instruction is necessary for installing all packages listed in package.json file
# Expose the port number – it should be excluded by default as we are not exposing ports to outside world, use EXPOSE <port> or CMD [ "node", ... ] if you want expose your application on a specific port (e.g., PORT=4926). Including an `EXPOSE` instruction is necessary for any service that needs network access and has exposed ports to outside world
# Define the default command – it should be excluded by default as we are not defining how docker will run our application, use CMD [ "node", ... ] if you want a different cmd/entrypoint. Including `CMD` instruction is necessary for any service that needs network access and has exposed ports to outside world
